title: Empirical Laws in Computer Science
summary: Covers the 90:10 law, Amdahl's law, temporal locality, and spatial locality – the fundamental empirical principles guiding computer architecture decisions.
content_points: 90:10 law (Knuth): 90% of execution time is spent in 10% of the instructions.
Amdahl's law: A(a, fa) = 1 / ((1-fa) + fa/a). Best gains come from optimising the most-used components.
Temporal locality: recently accessed memory locations will likely be accessed again soon.
Spatial locality: neighbours of recently accessed locations will likely be accessed soon.
Physical order = the order instructions are stored; logical order = the order they are executed (may differ due to jumps).
learning_objectives: Apply Amdahl's law to calculate system speedup
Explain the 90:10 law and its implications
